2606.30185.title: Dynamo: Dynamic Skill-Tool Evolution for Vision-Language Agents
2606.30185.paper_summary: Improving vision-language models (VLMs) on visual reasoning typically requires retraining or hand-designed prompts and tools. We present Dynamo, a training-free framework that adapts a frozen VLM without any weight updates. On a small labeled training subset, the agent inspects its own correct and incorrect attempts and evolves two complementary capabilities: reusable reasoning skills for cognitive bottlenecks, and executable visual tools for perceptual ones. Each generated tool is paired with a skill that specifies when to invoke it, and both capability types accumulate in a persistent library. Across four visual reasoning benchmarks and five VLM backbones, Dynamo improves direct inference on all 20 model--benchmark settings (avg. +5.6 acc). When the tool set is given in advance, the framework learns when to call each tool, and per-step tool choice improves on every tested backbone. Against task-specific RL (VTool-R1, DeepEyes), Dynamo closes 65--99% of the RL gap at a fraction of the compute, and combines additively with RL when available.
2606.30185.paper_summary_zh: 改善視覺-語言模型（VLMs）在視覺推理上的表現通常需要重新訓練或手動設計提示和工具。<lb/>我們提出了Dynamo，一個無需訓練的框架，能在不進行任何權重更新的情況下調整一個凍結的VLM。<lb/>在一個小型標記訓練子集上，代理檢查自己正確和不正確的嘗試，並發展出兩種互補的能力：針對認知瓶頸的可重用推理技能，以及針對感知瓶頸的可執行視覺工具。<lb/>每個生成的工具都與一項技能配對，該技能指定何時調用它，並且這兩種能力類型在持久庫中累積。<lb/>在四個視覺推理基準和五個VLM骨幹上，Dynamo在所有20個模型-基準設置中改善了直接推理（平均+5.6準確率）。<lb/>當工具集提前給出時，框架學習何時調用每個工具，每一步的工具選擇在每個測試的骨幹上都有所改善。<lb/>與特定任務的強化學習（VTool-R1，DeepEyes）相比，Dynamo在計算量的一小部分下縮小了65-99%的強化學習差距，並在可用時與強化學習進行加性結合。
2606.30185.author: Yutao Sun et.al.
2606.30185.authors: Yutao Sun, Yanting Miao, Hao-Xuan Ma, Mengyu Zhou, Mingshuai Chen, Tiancheng Zhao, Dexin Wang, Lei Lv, Li Xu, Xiaoxi Jiang, Guanjun Jiang
